name: Probiotics - Lactobacillus spp.
summary: Lactobacillus probiotics have moderate, strain-specific evidence for antibiotic-associated diarrhea prevention and modest IBS symptom improvement.
description: Lactobacillus probiotic category; effects are strain-, dose-, and indication-specific, involving colonization, immune crosstalk, and barrier-related signaling.
safetyNotes: Most studied probiotics are well tolerated in generally healthy people, but safety is strain- and host-specific. Rare invasive infections are a concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients.
contraindications: Use only with clinician oversight in severe immunocompromise
safety cannot be generalized across Lactobacillus strains.
